memory.summary: I need to reschedule my plan because our cohort is eating lunch together. I'll ask them if they can join the study session later.
Cohort_3 already knows I'm tired and wants to skip lunch, but they're insisting on meeting for dinner at 7 PM instead of our proposed study session.

My energy is critically low, forcing me to bluntly renegotiate the 6 PM deadline and skip dinner with cohort_3 to protect my well-being.
I just finished the analysis section but missed the deadline, and my energy is critically low. I need to tell them straight up that we can't do this.
I just told my close friends I missed the deadline, which feels heavy but necessary. Now I need to protect our trust by explaining this honestly before we reconnect.

My assertive nature requires me to state the 'no' firmly rather than letting them guess or pressure me further. By securing a concrete alternative now, I protect our group dynamic from resentment while maintaining my own standards.
